In all these methods our main difficulty lies in pin-point
ing the actual time of an event. The time when a certain
aspect takes place is not always the time when the event takes
place. There is always an interval between these two. An
aspect may be found on the first of July, but the event may
occur in the following April or May. This time lag has to be
deterrnined,if the time is corrected on the basis of the past
events, Moreover, this may not agree with the dasa, antar
dasa and Vidasa of the Visathan System .
.B efore any rectification is done, it is necessary to remem
ber a few factors. First we consider natural benefics and male
tics. Jupiter is a full benefic, Venus is three-quarters benefic,
Mercury is a half benefic, and the waning Moon is a quarter
benefic. The full Moon, the Moon on the tenth day of the
bright half and on the fifth day of the other half, and the half
Moon are benefics respectively as full, three-quarters, and
half.
